title: wolfhsm URI needs server-address component for multi-device support
description:
## Problem
The current URI secretx:wolfhsm:<label> has two design gaps that must be resolved before implementing the real C FFI layer.

## Gap 1: No device selector (multi-device)
wolfHSM architecture is client/server: each physical HSM runs a wolfHSM server daemon; clients connect over a transport (Unix socket, TCP, SPI, UART). With only a label in the URI there is no way to address a second device independently. Proposed: secretx:wolfhsm:<server-addr>/<id-or-label>[?label=<name>] where server-addr is a socket path or host:port (e.g. /var/run/wolfhsm0.sock or 192.168.1.10:8080). This mirrors pkcs11 using secretx:pkcs11:<slot>/<label>.

## Gap 2: wolfHSM NVM is addressed by numeric ID, not label
The wolfHSM NVM API uses whNvmId (uint16_t) as the primary key. The label field (24 bytes) is metadata stored with the object but is NOT used for lookup. There is no NvmFindByLabel API. Read (NvmRead) and destroy (NvmDestroyObjects) take numeric IDs. Write is NvmAddObject(id, access, flags, label, data). Update = NvmDestroyObjects([id]) + NvmAddObject(id, ...). To look up by label you must enumerate all object IDs via NvmList and call NvmGetMetadata per entry — O(n) over all NVM objects.

## Decision needed: URI path component and write semantics

Four options for the path component:

1. Numeric ID only — secretx:wolfhsm:<server>/<id> e.g. .../42. Fast O(1), matches native API exactly. Opaque in config; caller must know the numeric ID.

2. Label only, scan at runtime — secretx:wolfhsm:<server>/<label>. Human-friendly, matches current stub design. get() and put() require a linear scan of all NVM objects via NvmList + NvmGetMetadata to find the matching ID before each operation (unless cached). On embedded hardware with small NVM counts (tens to hundreds of objects) this may be acceptable if results are cached after first lookup.

3. Both ID and label — secretx:wolfhsm:<server>/<id>?label=<name>. ID in the path for O(1) lookup; label as an optional query parameter used only at creation time (passed to NvmAddObject so the object is self-describing on the device). No scan ever needed. SecretUri already supports arbitrary query params via param(key) — no core changes required. Trade-off: caller must know the numeric ID and put it in the config URI.
   - get():   NvmRead(id)                               — O(1)
   - put():   NvmDestroyObjects([id]) + NvmAddObject(id, ..., label, data) — O(1), label stored on device
   - sign():  wolfHSM keystore uses same numeric ID scheme — O(1)

4. Label only with startup cache — like option 2 but the backend scans once on first use, builds an in-memory label→id map, and reuses it for the lifetime of the backend object. Combines human-friendly URIs with amortised O(1) after first call. Cache is invalidated on put().

## Why now
Changing the URI format after real users exist is a breaking change. The stub is the right time to settle this.
notes:
wolfHSM source code is available at ~/GIT/wolfHSM (shallow clone). Key files for implementors:
- wolfhsm/wh_client.h       — full client API including wh_Client_Nvm* and wh_Client_Key* functions
- wolfhsm/wh_client_crypto.h — crypto key operations: EccSign, Ed25519Sign, RsaFunction, Curve25519SharedSecret, etc.
- wolfhsm/wh_keyid.h        — whKeyId bitfield layout: type[15:12] | user[11:8] | id[7:0]; WH_MAKE_KEYID macro
- wolfhsm/wh_common.h       — whNvmId (uint16_t), whNvmMetadata struct (id, label[24], access, flags, len)
- wolfhsm/wh_nvm.h          — server-side NVM interface and wh_Nvm_AddObjectChecked docs
- src/wh_client_nvm.c       — NVM client implementation
- src/wh_server_nvm.c       — NVM server implementation
- examples/demo/client/wh_demo_client_nvm.c — canonical usage example showing AddObject/Read/DestroyObjects pattern
wolfP11 source is also at ~/GIT/wolfP11 and ~/WORK/wolfP11 for PKCS#11 reference.

## Two secret namespaces — NVM objects vs keystore

wolfHSM has five secret-holding namespaces. Only two matter for secretx:

  KEYTYPE_NVM=0x0    whNvmId (flat uint16)          SecretStore::get/put — arbitrary data blobs
  KEYTYPE_CRYPTO=0x1 whKeyId (type|user|id bitfield) SigningBackend::sign — ECC, Ed25519, RSA keys
  KEYTYPE_SHE=0x2    whKeyId                         automotive AUTOSAR SHE spec — out of scope
  KEYTYPE_COUNTER=0x3 whNvmId                        monotonic counters — not secrets
  Certificates        whNvmId                        trusted CA store — possible future, out of scope

NVM objects and crypto keys are DIFFERENT namespaces. A plain whNvmId of 42 is NOT the same as
WH_MAKE_KEYID(WH_KEYTYPE_CRYPTO, 0, 42). The current stub conflates both under one label field.

The real implementation resolves the ID differently depending on which trait is being invoked:
  get()/put()                 -> plain whNvmId via NvmRead / NvmAddObject
  sign()/public_key_der()     -> WH_MAKE_KEYID(WH_KEYTYPE_CRYPTO, 0, id) via wh_Client_EccSign /
                                 wh_Client_Ed25519Sign / wh_Client_RsaFunction

The URI does NOT need a type field — the trait method called determines which namespace to use.
This must be explicitly documented in the implementation.
